title: France and Spain face off in the World Cup semifinals
summary: The match between two world football powers generates great expectations due to their playing style and young stars.

title: Inflation in the United States moderates to 3.5% annually in June
summary: The U.S. CPI shows a greater-than-expected slowdown, although gasoline prices continue to push upwards.

title: Trump hardens his stance against Iran with naval blockade and attacks in the Gulf
summary: The U.S. President withdraws the transit tax proposal and orders the blockade of Iranian ports, raising tensions in the Middle East.

title: Japan suffers extreme heat wave with thousands of heatstroke emergencies
summary: High temperatures have caused more than 4,500 emergency transfers and at least one death, while the media launch prevention campaigns.

title: Prime Minister Takachi leads recruitment of Liberal Democratic Party affiliates
summary: Takachi surpasses her internal rivals in number of new members, while the party registers a general drop in its membership.

title: KFC in Japan closes its stores due to a computer failure at its logistics provider
summary: The fast-food chain temporarily halts its operations and online order reception due to a problem in Nichirei's system.

title: Pacific bluefin tuna summit concludes without agreement to expand fishing quotas
summary: Mexico opposed at the last moment, preventing the quota increase, which will continue to be debated in future meetings.

title: Fukuoka assembly vice-president admits to being the voice in an alleged extortion audio
summary: The politician acknowledged that the recording contains his words, although he denied receiving money; the investigation continues.

title: Kyodo News agency rectifies its coverage on the Hino-cho case and sanctions 11 journalists
summary: The news agency withdrew an article about the review trial and took disciplinary measures against its team.

title: Yen depreciates against dollar while euro rises on Fed expectations
summary: In the currency market, the yen falls to 162 per dollar and the euro strengthens due to the possible pause in rate hikes.

title: Thrilling day in Japanese professional baseball with milestones and outstanding results
summary: Softbank reaches 50 wins, Sakamoto's homerun ties the game, and Masaki gets four hits.

title: Health alert in 27 Japanese prefectures due to hand, foot and mouth disease epidemic
summary: The number of cases exceeds the warning level, especially in Tokyo where infections double the threshold.

title: Sumo wrestler Fujinokawa achieves four consecutive victories against yokozuna
summary: Fujinokawa becomes the first wrestler in the modern era to win four consecutive bouts against champions.

title: Japanese government debates change in GPIF pension fund investment strategy
summary: The Finance Minister pushes for greater investment in domestic assets, while the Health Minister calls for caution.

title: Lower House approves secondary capital bill with uncertain future in Senate
summary: The government achieves a key step in its legislative agenda, but still does not guarantee a majority in the upper house.

title: Prime Minister Takachi meets with political allies at working dinner
summary: Takachi, known for her little fondness for dinners, participated briefly in a meeting with lawmakers who supported her.

title: Centrist party Nakatada presents its vision of government focused on competitive welfare
summary: The opposition group seeks to offer an alternative to Takachi's government with social and economic policies.

title: Nakatada, Ritsumin and Komei advance negotiations for a possible merger
summary: Opposition parties explore integration mechanisms to strengthen their electoral position.

title: Figure skaters Uno Shoma and Honda Marin hold public training session
summary: The Japanese pair showed their new choreographies and talked about their goals for the upcoming season.

title: Ukraine faces political crisis with cabinet resignation while army conducts drone operation
summary: The Ukrainian government resigns amid internal power struggles, while the armed forces carry out an unmanned amphibious attack.
